name: Danish Pastries - Pan-1
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Butter or margarine for folding
1/4 - 1/3 cup Canned fruit filling or preserves
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the first five ingredients in the bread pan with water. Place dry yeast in the yeast holder. Set to BASIC DOUGH mode and start.
(Breadmaker completes basic dough mode in 2 hours and 25 minutes)
2. Transfer dough to a greased bowl. Cover. Let rest at room temperature for 30 minutes.
3. Roll out chilled butter between two sheets of waxed paper into an 8 x 7 inch rectangle. Return to the refrigerator and chill for at least 1 hour.
4. On a lightly floured surface, roll dough to a 10 x 10 1/2 inches rectangle.
5. Spread about two-thirds of the dough with butter. Place third without butter over the center third.
6. Fold remaining third on top. Seal edges. Let rest in refrigerator for 20-30 minutes.
7. Turn dough at right angles and roll to a 10 x 10 1/2 inches rectangle. Fold into thirds, wrapping tightly and chilling again for 20-30 minutes.
Repeat this folding process three more times, chilling the dough after each rolling.
8. Chill several hours or overnight.
9. Roll dough to 10 x 10 1/2 inches, then cut into 6 squares. Roll each square into a 7-inch circle.
10. Place filling in center of each square and brush corners with water. Fold opposite corners over the center and press all four corners tightly together.
11. Transfer to a greased baking pan. Spray top lightly. Bake at 357°F for 10-15 minutes or until golden brown.
Note: Ensure ingredients are measured accurately and follow the basic dough mode as specified.
